hero.question: Will Rory McIlroy win the 2026 Masters tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 56.5%, the market is basically flipping a coin on whether a 36-year-old golfer wins a major four years from now, which is either genius humility or lazy pricing given McIlroy's track record and Augusta's geometry favoring his game. The 27-point surge suggests someone knows something about his near-term form or injury status, so this is a fade play until you see fresh data confirming the optimism.

daily_take.headline: McIlroy's Masters odds just jumped 27 points. The smart money knows something.
daily_take.deck: Rory's back-to-back wins and improved form triggered a 27-point surge to 56.5%, making him the overwhelming favorite. But at near-evens odds, you're pricing in perfection over 72 holes against a field that includes Scheffler, Rahm, and Koepka. The real edge is selling here. A 56.5% price for any golfer assumes zero catastrophic rounds, perfect weather, and zero luck breaks going wrong. Fade the recency bias.
